revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Ishii Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = [[Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[Mishima District (Niigata)|Mishima District]], [[Izumozaki, Niigata|Izumozaki Town]], Ishiimachi 583 |coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|32|34.3|N|138|41|15.9|E|region:JP|name=石井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor) {{ill|Disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage stops = |Annual festival = [[June 17]]–[[June 18]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|lat_deg=37|lat_min=32|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=41|lon_sec=15.9|label=Ishii Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=}}{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building|frame-width=200}} }} '''Ishii Shrine''' (石井神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[Mishima District (Niigata)|Mishima District]], [[Izumozaki, Niigata|Izumozaki Town]], Ishiimachi. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} {{ill|Disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}}. The shrine is regarded as the total shrine of Izumozaki Town. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. According to legend, during the {{ill|Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|ja|神代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, who sought to pacify {{ill|Sado Island|en|Sado Island|qid=Q998623|lt=Sado Island|1=Sado Island}}, sprinkled water from a stone well, causing twelve sacred trees to grow overnight. These trees were used to construct a ship to cross to Sado. A great deity of maritime protection was enshrined at the site of these sacred trees, known as Iibana. For this reason, the shrine was originally called {{ill|Jūnisho Shrine|ja|十二所神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnisho Shrine|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, prayers by the {{ill|Sado bugyō|en|Sado bugyō|qid=Q4274645|lt=Sado governor|1=Sado bugyō}} calmed a storm, enabling safe return. This increased faith in the shrine, which was relocated from {{ill|Mt. Jūni|ja|十二山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Jūni|qq=}} in Kanazawa Village to its current site in 712 CE (Wadō 4).{{Cite news |title=Izumozaki: Hoketsu Materials |newspaper=Sato Bookstore |date=1906-09-01 |author=Nishizawa Shinji (ed.) |page=}} == deity == {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|qid=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|Keigai-Sessha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|qq=}}) == Festivals == Since 1690 CE (Genroku 3), the shrine has held the {{ill|Maritime Safety Prayer Festival|ja|海上安全祈願祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maritime Safety Prayer Festival|qq=}} on June 17–18. Later, this was conducted jointly with other shrines, and in 2010 (Heisei 22), it was renamed the {{ill|Izumozaki Grand Festival|ja|出雲崎大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumozaki Grand Festival|qq=}}. A mikoshi procession visits Ryōkandō, Anase, Iibana, and returns to Ryōkandō. == Visitor Information == '''Access''' * Rail: From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[Izumozaki Station]], [[Kashiwazaki Station]], or [[Nagaoka Station]], take the {{ill|Echigo Kotsu|en|Echigo Kotsu|qid=Q11636491|lt=Echigo Kotsu|1=Echigo Kotsu}} bus bound for Izumozaki Garage and alight at "Ryōkandō-mae" bus stop. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|石井神社 (出雲崎町)|version=102871573|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Ishii Shrine (Izumozaki) (Q123118312)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ishii Shrine (Izumozaki)|lt=Iwai Shrine|ja|石井神社 (出雲崎町)|qid=Q123118312|1=Iwai Shrine (Izumozaki)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || — || {{ill|Ihawino Shrine|lt=Ihawino Shrine|qid=Q135040453|1=Ihawino Shrine}} || {{coord|37.542861|138.687750|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182442 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q123118312}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ishii Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, [[Mishima District (Niigata)|Mishima District]], [[:en:Izumozaki, Niigata|Izumozaki Town]], Ishiimachi 583 |coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|32|34.3|N|138|41|15.9|E|region:JP|name=石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage stops = |Annual festival = [[June 17]]–[[June 18]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|lat_deg=37|lat_min=32|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=41|lon_sec=15.9|label=Ishii Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=}}{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building|frame-width=200}} }} '''Ishii Shrine''' (石井神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, [[Mishima District (Niigata)|Mishima District]], [[:en:Izumozaki, Niigata|Izumozaki Town]], Ishiimachi. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}. The shrine is regarded as the total shrine of Izumozaki Town. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. According to legend, during the {{ill|Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who sought to pacify {{ill|Sado Island|en|Sado Island|qid=Q998623|lt=Sado Island|1=Sado Island}}, sprinkled water from a stone well, causing twelve sacred trees to grow overnight. These trees were used to construct a ship to cross to Sado. A great deity of maritime protection was enshrined at the site of these sacred trees, known as Iibana. For this reason, the shrine was originally called {{ill|Jūnisho Shrine|ja|十二所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnisho Shrine|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, prayers by the {{ill|Sado bugyō|en|Sado bugyō|qid=Q4274645|lt=Sado governor|1=Sado bugyō}} calmed a storm, enabling safe return. This increased faith in the shrine, which was relocated from {{ill|Mt. Jūni|ja|十二山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Jūni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町横室)|1=Akagi Shrine (Fujimi Town Yokomuro)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131275527}} in Kanazawa Village to its current site in 712 CE (Wadō 4).{{Cite news |title=Izumozaki: Hoketsu Materials |newspaper=Sato Bookstore |date=1906-09-01 |author=Nishizawa Shinji (ed.) |page=}} == deity == {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|qid=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|Keigai-Sessha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == Festivals == Since 1690 CE (Genroku 3), the shrine has held the {{ill|Maritime Safety Prayer Festival|ja|海上安全祈願祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maritime Safety Prayer Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on June 17–18. Later, this was conducted jointly with other shrines, and in 2010 (Heisei 22), it was renamed the {{ill|Izumozaki Grand Festival|ja|出雲崎大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumozaki Grand Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A mikoshi procession visits Ryōkandō, Anase, Iibana, and returns to Ryōkandō. == Visitor Information == '''Access''' * Rail: From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Izumozaki Station|en|Izumozaki Station|qid=Q6102426|lt=Izumozaki Station|1=Izumozaki Station}}, {{ill|Kashiwazaki Station|en|Kashiwazaki Station|qid=Q5353481|lt=Kashiwazaki Station|1=Kashiwazaki Station}}, or {{ill|Nagaoka Station|en|Nagaoka Station|qid=Q1138252|lt=Nagaoka Station|1=Nagaoka Station}}, take the {{ill|Echigo Kotsu|en|Echigo Kotsu|qid=Q11636491|lt=Echigo Kotsu|1=Echigo Kotsu}} bus bound for Izumozaki Garage and alight at "Ryōkandō-mae" bus stop. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|石井神社 (出雲崎町)|version=102871573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q123118312}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ishii Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, [[Mishima District (Niigata)|Mishima District]], [[:en:Izumozaki, Niigata|Izumozaki Town]], Ishiimachi 583 |coordinates = {{ill|coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|32|34.3|N|138|41|15.9|E|region:JP|name=石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{ill|Disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage stops = |Annual festival = [[June 17]]–[[June 18]] |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Niigata|lat_deg=37|lat_min=32|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=138|lon_min=41|lon_sec=15.9|label=Ishii Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=}}{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building|frame-width=200}} }} '''Ishii Shrine''' (石井神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, [[Mishima District (Niigata)|Mishima District]], [[:en:Izumozaki, Niigata|Izumozaki Town]], Ishiimachi. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|Disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}. The shrine is regarded as the total shrine of Izumozaki Town. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Overview == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. According to legend, during the {{ill|Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, who sought to pacify {{ill|Sado Island|en|Sado Island|qid=Q998623|lt=Sado Island|1=Sado Island}}, sprinkled water from a stone well, causing twelve sacred trees to grow overnight. These trees were used to construct a ship to cross to Sado. A great deity of maritime protection was enshrined at the site of these sacred trees, known as Iibana. For this reason, the shrine was originally called {{ill|Jūnisho Shrine|ja|十二所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnisho Shrine|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, prayers by the {{ill|Sado bugyō|en|Sado bugyō|qid=Q4274645|lt=Sado governor|1=Sado bugyō}} calmed a storm, enabling safe return. This increased faith in the shrine, which was relocated from {{ill|Mt. Jūni|ja|十二山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Jūni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町横室)|1=Akagi Shrine (Fujimi Town Yokomuro)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131275527}} in Kanazawa Village to its current site in 712 CE (Wadō 4).{{Cite news |title=Izumozaki: Hoketsu Materials |newspaper=Sato Bookstore |date=1906-09-01 |author=Nishizawa Shinji (ed.) |page=}} == deity == {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|qid=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|Keigai-Sessha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == Festivals == Since 1690 CE (Genroku 3), the shrine has held the {{ill|Maritime Safety Prayer Festival|ja|海上安全祈願祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maritime Safety Prayer Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on June 17–18. Later, this was conducted jointly with other shrines, and in 2010 (Heisei 22), it was renamed the {{ill|Izumozaki Grand Festival|ja|出雲崎大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumozaki Grand Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A mikoshi procession visits Ryōkandō, Anase, Iibana, and returns to Ryōkandō. == Visitor Information == '''Access''' * Rail: From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Izumozaki Station|en|Izumozaki Station|qid=Q6102426|lt=Izumozaki Station|1=Izumozaki Station}}, {{ill|Kashiwazaki Station|en|Kashiwazaki Station|qid=Q5353481|lt=Kashiwazaki Station|1=Kashiwazaki Station}}, or {{ill|Nagaoka Station|en|Nagaoka Station|qid=Q1138252|lt=Nagaoka Station|1=Nagaoka Station}}, take the {{ill|Echigo Kotsu|en|Echigo Kotsu|qid=Q11636491|lt=Echigo Kotsu|1=Echigo Kotsu}} bus bound for Izumozaki Garage and alight at "Ryōkandō-mae" bus stop. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|石井神社 (出雲崎町)|version=102871573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q123118312}} ==Notes== [[da:d:Q16277320]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izunozaki Town]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Izumozaki]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumozaki]] [[Category:出雲崎町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] ==merged content== {{Expand Japanese|石井神社 (出雲崎町)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ishii Shrine (Izumozaki) | native_name = 石井神社 (出雲崎町) | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ishii Shrine (Izumozaki) | coordinates = {{coord|37.542861|138.68775}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Iwai Shrine'''|石井神社 (出雲崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो code (P281) == * 949-4306https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2110005006806 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Ihawino Shrine|lt=Ihawino Shrine|qid=Q135040453|1=Ihawino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '新潟県三島郡出雲崎町石井町583', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '新潟県出雲崎町石井町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:石井神社 (出雲崎町)]] {{wikidata link|Q123118312}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ishii Shrine |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Mishima District, Niigata}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima District (Niigata)|Q1155567|qid=Q1155567|lt=Mishima District|1=Santō District, Niigata}}{{ill|lt=Izumozaki|Q1204583|qid=Q1204583|1=Izumozaki}} Ishii-chō 583 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|32|34.3|N|138|41|15.9|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Ishii Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=June 17|Q2672|qid=Q2672|1=June 17}} / {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|qid=Q2670|1=June 18}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Ishii Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ishii Shrine''' (いわいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishii-chō, {{ill|lt=Izumozaki|Q1204583|qid=Q1204583|1=Izumozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Mishima District (Niigata)|Q1155567|qid=Q1155567|lt=Mishima District|1=Santō District, Niigata}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It is the sōsha of {{ill|lt=Izumozaki|Q1204583|qid=Q1204583|1=Izumozaki}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === The founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Age of the Gods (Japanese mythology)|Q70447980|qid=Q70447980|lt=Age of the Gods|1=Age of the Gods}}, when Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto sought to pacify {{ill|lt=Sado Island|Q998623|qid=Q998623|lt=Sado|1=Sado Island}}, he sprinkled water from a stone well and built a ship overnight from twelve sacred trees that grew at once, then crossed to Sado. At that time, a great sea-protecting deity was enshrined at Ihana, where those twelve sacred trees stood. For that reason, it was originally called Jūnisho Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, the {{ill|lt=Sado bugyō|Q4274645|qid=Q4274645|1=Sado bugyō}} prayed there and was able to return safely after a storm subsided; devotion deepened, and in {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=712|Q23521|qid=Q23521|1=712}}), the shrine was moved from Jūniyama in Ihana, Kanazawa Village, to its present site.{{Cite news |title=出雲崎：北越資料 |newspaper=Sato Bookstore |date=1906-09-01 |author=Compiled by Nishizawa Shinji |page=}} === Enshrined deity === {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|qid=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q1071160|qid=Q1071160|lt=outside subsidiary shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}}) === Festivals === From {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1690|Q7678|qid=Q7678|1=1690}}), it has been held on June 17 and June 18 as the “Maritime Safety Prayer Festival.” Later it came to be held jointly with other shrines, and from {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 22 it was renamed the “Izumozaki Grand Festival.” The mikoshi goes from Ryōkandō around Amase and Ihana and returns to Ryōkandō. === Local information === '''Access''' * Rail: from {{ill|lt=Izumozaki Station|Q6102426|qid=Q6102426|1=Izumozaki Station}}, {{ill|lt=Kashiwazaki Station|Q5353481|qid=Q5353481|1=Kashiwazaki Station}}, or {{ill|lt=Nagaoka Station|Q1138252|qid=Q1138252|1=Nagaoka Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|qid=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}}, take {{ill|lt=Echigo Kōtsū|Q11636491|qid=Q11636491|1=Echigo Kotsu}} bound for Izumozaki depot, get off at “Ryōkandō-mae” === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ishii Shrine (Izumozaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ishii Shrine (Izumozaki)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
